key_questions.question: Why did the Croatti family / UniFirst board agree in Mar 2026 after unanimously rejecting Cintas's $275 cash bid ~14 months earlier — was it the Engine Capital activist campaign / proxy fight, the higher cash+stock price, or a governance/leadership change?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Determines whether this is a value-maximizing arm's-length outcome or a pressured one, and what that says about price adequacy and deal certainty.

key_questions.question: What is the true 'unaffected' price the ~$310 premium should be measured against, given a publicly-known prior approach (Jan 2025) and an active Engine Capital campaign that likely already embedded takeover/sale speculation into UNF's stock?
key_questions.why_it_matters: If the baseline is contaminated by prior bid + activist speculation, the headline premium overstates the real control premium — central to whether Cintas overpaid.

key_questions.question: Through what specific operating lever (route density, plant/branch utilization, SG&A, procurement) does Cintas close UniFirst's margin gap — UNF's operating margin (~7-8%) is roughly a third of Cintas's — and how much of the $375M synergy is cost vs revenue?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Cost synergies on a density lever are far more realizable than revenue synergies; the lever is the whole value case for paying up.

key_questions.question: Does the ~$375M cost-synergy target (run-rate within 4 years) hold up against UniFirst's own cost base — it is roughly 2x UNF's FY25 operating income (~$184M) — and what is the ex-synergy acquisition multiple?
key_questions.why_it_matters: A synergy figure that dwarfs the target's standalone profit needs a hard mechanism, or the deal only works on heroic execution.

key_questions.question: Given a combined entity approaching ~half the U.S. uniform-rental market and the FTC Second Request (Jun 11, 2026) plus competitor outreach, what is the realistic remedy path — local route/branch divestitures vs. a block — and the base rate for route-based/facility-services deals?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Antitrust is the dominant close-risk; the remedy path drives both deal certainty and the economics of what Cintas actually keeps.

key_questions.question: How does the merger agreement allocate the antitrust risk — is there a regulatory-efforts / 'hell or high water' covenant, a divestiture cap, a reverse termination fee payable if the FTC blocks it, and what is the outside / drop-dead date?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Sets who bears the Second-Request risk and how long Cintas is committed to fight — a key input to the close-likelihood force balance.

key_questions.question: How did the 2023 Aramark→Vestis spinoff and Vestis's subsequent struggles reshape the competitive landscape, and is Cintas's 2026 move offensive (extend its lead) or defensive (consolidate #3 before a weakened rival or PE buyer can)?
key_questions.why_it_matters: The 'why now' trigger; distinguishes a strategic density play from opportunistic empire-building.

key_questions.question: What do the cash/stock mix and the $2.85B bridge + revolver do to Cintas's pro-forma leverage (~1.5x net debt/EBITDA cited) and EPS — is the deal accretive or dilutive once new-debt cost and the 0.7720x shares issued are weighed against synergy phasing?
key_questions.why_it_matters: Tests whether the financial structure is conservative or stretched, and whether accretion depends on the synergies actually landing.

key_questions.question: Is UniFirst's underperformance (the Engine Capital thesis: weak TSR under family control) a fixable execution/operating gap Cintas can close, or a structural disadvantage — i.e., is the synergy case really 'UNF was under-managed'?
key_questions.why_it_matters: If the gap is fixable by a better operator, the synergy lever is credible; if structural, the premium is harder to justify.

planned_sources.title: UniFirst SEC filings (EDGAR, CIK 0000717954)

planned_sources.title: UniFirst FY2025 Q4 / full-year earnings 8-K (Ex-99.1)
planned_sources.rationale: Discovery: FY25 revenue ~$2.43B and operating income ~$184.5M — the standalone earnings base the premium and synergy are measured against.

planned_sources.title: Cintas SEC filings (EDGAR, CIK 0000723254)

planned_sources.search_hint: Cintas 10-K fiscal 2025 EDGAR CIK 723254 (segment revenue mix, operating margin ~22%, strategy)
planned_sources.rationale: Acquirer profile + the margin/operating benchmark UniFirst would be brought up to (the synergy lever).

planned_sources.title: Cintas + UniFirst 10-K 'Competition' sections

classes_skipped.reason: No material standalone litigation surfaced in discovery. Merger-objection shareholder suits (if any) will be captured via 8-K / proxy disclosure (see s_unf_legal); escalate to a dedicated docket pull only if research surfaces a material case.

Not skipped as a source of facts, but down-weighted: industry trade outlets (TRSA, American Laundry News, Textile Rental Services Association) corroborate the deal narrative and industry colour; load-bearing facts are taken from the SEC primaries above rather than trade press.
